questions.question: Define a database view and explain its key characteristics.
keyPoints: View is a virtual table based on a stored SQL query
Does not store data physically - computed at access time
Provides data security by restricting access
Enables simplification of complex queries
Reflects changes in base tables dynamically

questions.question: Explain the different types of views based on their updatability.
keyPoints: Updatable views: Allow DML operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
Must be based on single table without aggregates/GROUP BY/DISTINCT
Read-only views: Do not permit DML operations
Materialized views: Store physical copy of query results
Complex views with JOINs are typically read-only

questions.question: Describe the components of ODBC architecture.
keyPoints: Application layer: Makes ODBC calls
Driver Manager: Loads appropriate driver, manages connections
ODBC Driver: Translates ODBC calls to DBMS-specific commands
Data Source: Contains database connection information
Each layer communicates with adjacent layers only

questions.question: What are the three types of Data Source Names (DSN)? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: User DSN: User-specific, visible only to current user, stored locally
System DSN: Available to all users on the system, requires admin privileges to configure
File DSN: Stored in .dsn file, portable across machines, can be shared

questions.question: Explain any five advantages of using database views.
keyPoints: Data security: Restrict access to sensitive columns/rows
Query simplification: Hide complex queries from users
Data independence: External schema abstraction
Logical data independence: Change schema without affecting applications
Customized presentation: Show user-specific data
Integrity enforcement: Define constraints at view level

questions.question: List conditions under which a view becomes non-updatable in SQL.
keyPoints: Contains aggregate functions (SUM, AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN)
Uses GROUP BY or HAVING clause
Contains DISTINCT keyword
Uses subqueries in WHERE clause
Involves multiple base tables (JOIN)
Contains calculated columns
Does not include all NOT NULL columns of base table

questions.question: What is Open Database Connectivity (ODBC)? List its advantages.
keyPoints: ODBC is a standard API for accessing multiple DBMS
Enables application-database portability
Provides uniform interface across different databases
Separates application logic from DBMS-specific code
Reduces development complexity and cost
Supports connection to heterogeneous databases

questions.question: Explain the concept of database views in detail. How do simple views differ from complex views? Discuss updatable vs non-updatable views with examples.
keyPoints: Definition and nature of views as virtual tables
Simple views: single table, no aggregates, can be updatable
Complex views: multiple tables, aggregates, JOINs - typically read-only
Updatable view conditions with examples
Materialized views as special case storing physical data
Practical examples of CREATE VIEW statements
Security and query simplification benefits

questions.question: Explain ODBC architecture in detail. Discuss the role of ODBC Driver Manager and ODBC Drivers. How does ODBC achieve database interoperability?
keyPoints: Four-layer architecture explanation
Application layer functions
Driver Manager responsibilities and loading process
ODBC Driver translation mechanism
Data Source configuration and types
Connection process using SQLConnect/SQLDriverConnect
How ODBC achieves DBMS independence
Advantages in enterprise environments

questions.question: Write SQL statements to: (a) Create a view showing employee details with salary > 50000 (b) Create a view with aggregate data (c) Drop a view (d) Explain how views provide security. Include examples with syntax.
keyPoints: CREATE VIEW for simple query with WHERE clause
CREATE VIEW with GROUP BY for aggregated data
DROP VIEW syntax and behavior
Security through column/row restriction
Example demonstrating different view types
Explanation of WITH CHECK OPTION
Discussion on when views become invalid

questions.question: Compare and contrast: (a) Simple View vs Complex View (b) Updatable vs Non-updatable View (c) Materialized vs Regular View (d) User DSN vs System DSN vs File DSN
keyPoints: Simple vs Complex: table count, functions, updatability
Updatable vs Non-updatable: DML support, conditions
Materialized vs Regular: storage, refresh requirements
DSN types: accessibility, configuration, portability
Practical implications of each comparison
